the_signal.headline: AI cost breakeven pushed to 2029—your ROI math is wrong
the_signal.body: Tom Tunguz's analysis shows AI spend won't hit ROI payoff until 2029 for most orgs, meaning you're selling efficiency gains to customers who won't see them for 3+ years. This fundamentally breaks your value prop narrative if you're pitching "immediate productivity wins." GTM teams need to rebuild deals around cost-per-outcome, not cost-per-token.

tool_drop.name: Edgee Claude Code Compressor V2
tool_drop.what: Compresses Claude context window usage to fit more code in fewer tokens.
tool_drop.why: If AI breakeven is 2029, every token saved directly improves your unit economics and competitive pricing—this is no longer nice-to-have.

thread_of_day.title: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol: Enterprise AI Orchestration at Scale
thread_of_day.takeaway: A2A protocol moves from experimental to specification-level—orgs building multi-agent revenue systems now need cross-agent communication patterns baked into architecture from day one.

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills

whats_moving.context: n8n's 5-level framework gives leadership a language to assess AI adoption—GTM teams can now map where their customers sit and sell stage-appropriate solutions.
Enterprise buyers are demanding spec-level documentation for agent-to-agent communication; this is shifting from vendor lock-in to open orchestration.
With 2029 breakeven reality, compression and smart retry architectures become differentiators—companies ignoring this will lose margin wars.

act_on_this: Audit your ROI narratives today—if you're selling immediate productivity gains, reframe around cost-per-qualified-outcome by 2029, not token savings by Q4.

numbers.context: This 3-year gap means your customers are buying on faith, not math—your GTM narrative needs to shift from efficiency to transformation ROI.
